title: Friday Sermon 29 March 2002
description:
Continued with the series of sermons on the Divine attribute of al-Aziz today, with reference to Quranic verses, and elucidating the verses with the Ahadith and various commentaries of holy and venerable persons.  

Referring to the incidence of Hudaybiyya at the time when the Companions of the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) were grief-stricken Allah the Exalted revealed verses promising triumph and tranquillity and later indeed fulfilled His promises:*

Primarily the dominance of the prophets of God is based on the reasoning that they present to people - some use force to be dominant, however, prophets of God have His help who is Dominant over all else that exists, He is Independent and all else is dependent on Him. 
  
The word Aziz connotes a Being that is Matchless and Mighty.

(Fakhruddin al-Razi.  'Tafsir e Kabir: Mafatih al-Ghayb').

The dominance of the prophets of God materializes in their aims and objectives being fulfilled, (Abu Althana Shihabuddin Mahmud al-Alusi.  'Ruh al-Ma'ani fi Tafsir al-Quran al-Azim wa al-Sabu al Mathānī').

The Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) said that Allah the Exalted has made it His tradition that His messengers prevail over others and that he was sent as a prophet in subordination of and completely absorbed in the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him).

(Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad.  'Nuzul al-Masih'.  Punjab Press, 1894.  'Ruhani Khazain: xviii'.  380, 381).

Briefly regarding the institution of Shura or the consultative bodies within the Ahmadiyya Community that are due to meet worldwide.  No one consulted their companions more than the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) did, who laid great stress on the significance and magnitude of collective counsel.

(Abu Isa Muhammad ibn Isa al-Tirmidhi.  'Jami al-Tirmidhi').

The Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) also always convened his companions for consultation and enjoined on the absoluteness of abiding by the counsel of the Shura. 

* Ahmad ibn Hanbal.  'Musnad Ahmad ibn Hanbal'.

The text https://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST20020329-UR.pdf
